title: C++ compilation error for unpacked array ports with -fno-inline
body:
## Bug

With `-fno-inline`, Verilator generates mismatched C++ types for unpacked array ports. The root wrapper emits `VL_OUT8` macros producing raw C arrays (`CData[256]`), while the module class uses `vlArgType()` producing `VlUnpacked<CData, 256>`. This type mismatch causes C++ compilation failure when the root wrapper copies port values from the module class.

```
error: incompatible types in assignment of 'VlUnpacked<CData, 256>' to 'CData [256]'
```

### Failing tests (1)

- `t_opt_slice_element_limit`

### Reproducer

```bash
cd test_regress
verilator --cc -fno-inline t/t_opt_slice_element_limit.v
make -C obj_dir -f Vt_opt_slice_element_limit.mk
# -> error: incompatible types in assignment
# Without -fno-inline, compiles successfully
```

### Related

- Found during the `-fno-inline` audit for #1539 (post-scope inliner)

### Version

Tested on master at `bb0e1c8c6` (`Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-152-gbb0e1c8c6`).
code_blocks:
error: incompatible types in assignment of 'VlUnpacked<CData, 256>' to 'CData [256]'
cd test_regress
verilator --cc -fno-inline t/t_opt_slice_element_limit.v
make -C obj_dir -f Vt_opt_slice_element_limit.mk
# -> error: incompatible types in assignment
# Without -fno-inline, compiles successfully
